c74object.name: tap.808.cowbell~
c74object.digest: TR-808-style cowbell voice, synthesized from the analog circuit
c74object.description:
The tap.808.cowbell~ object is a circuit-informed model of the Roland TR-808 cowbell: the metal bank's factory-trimmed 540 and 800 Hz square-wave pair, gated through the hardware's two-slope envelope — an abrupt initial decay that emphasizes the attack, then a softer ~33 ms tail — and voiced by the ~860 Hz filter from the schematic. Trigger with a signal rising edge (amplitude 0..1 = accent) or bang / trigger messages. The tuning bend brings the hardware's internal trimpots out as an attribute.
The eight tap.808 voices share one accent-bus contract; the family overview patcher (tap.808.maxhelp) demonstrates them together on a running pattern.

inlet.digest: Trigger: a rising edge fires the voice; edge amplitude (0..1) is the accent

method.name: signal
method.description: A rising edge triggers the cowbell, sample-accurately; edge amplitude (0..1) is the accent.

method.name: bang
method.description: Trigger at full accent (from the scheduler).

method.name: trigger
method.description: Trigger with an accent value (0..1, default 1).

attribute.name: level
attribute.digest: Output level (panel knob VR5 — the hardware's only control)

attribute.name: tuning
attribute.digest: Circuit bend: pitch ratio of the 540/800 Hz pair (default 1 = stock)
attribute.description: 0.25..4 — the internal factory trimpots, brought out.

attribute.name: tolerance
attribute.description: 0..1; 1 is the full ~20% production tolerance. Deterministic per seed.

attribute.name: seed
attribute.digest: Unit identity for the tolerance spread
attribute.description: Seed (>= 1); each seed is a different unit off the line.
